entry.title: Recommended way to retrieve an OTR long text by alias in ABAP
entry.content:
Hi,
with transactions SOTR_EDIT and SE63 it is possible to provide texts and translations. These texts can be loaded in ABAP code using function modules. When creating a new entry in SOTR_EDIT you have to choose between short and long text. When retrieving the texts in ABAP everything is working fine with short texts - but not with long texts.
 
Method CL_WD_UTILITIES=>GET_OTR_TEXT_BY_ALIAS works for both short and long texts. But the description of the method contains a warning ("DO NOT USE").
 
Function module SOTR_GET_TEXT_KEY can only retrieve short texts.
 
Function module SOTR_STRING_READ_TEXT_WITH_KEY could read long text, but you have to provide the concept id.
The function module SOTR_GET_KEY_FOR_ALIAS can retrieve concept ids for short text, but not for long text.
 
So I'm stuck here and I'd  like to know what is the recommended way to retrieve an OTR long text for an alias in ABAP? Which methods or function modules do you use?
 
Kind regards
Guido

entry.title: Re: Modernize SAP PI/PO Landscapes with SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Good topic, especially because many organizations are now reaching the point where maintaining older PI/PO landscapes is becoming more difficult than modernizing them.

In several SAP programs I’ve seen, integration complexity tends to grow quietly over the years — multiple interfaces, custom mappings, monitoring gaps, and aging middleware dependencies eventually become operational challenges during S/4HANA transformations or cloud adoption initiatives.

What I liked about the article is that it focuses not only on technical migration, but also on simplifying integration architecture and improving long-term maintainability. Moving toward Integration Suite feels less like a pure platform upgrade and more like an opportunity to modernize how integrations are monitored, governed, and scaled across hybrid environments.

The transition path is still a major concern for many customers though, especially for organizations with large PI/PO footprints and heavily customized interfaces. Practical migration guidance like this is definitely useful.

entry.title: Re: SFPLU-10849 | Manage Super Admin Access Creates Learn...
entry.content: Hi @Sunny, A super administrator is a privileged user responsible for setting up the instance or inviting others to do so. The first user created after instance provisioning is automatically granted super administrator rights.  Based on this definition, super administrators will be granted permissions accordingly. We adhere to a minimal privilege policy while ensuring a smooth customer onboarding experience, reducing manual steps and accommodating users with limited SuccessFactors knowledge.  The Admin Overseer, formerly the RBP Super Admin, is a restricted admin role dedicated to admin user management, with no operational responsibilities, and access to Manage RBP Admin Access and Manage Super Admin Access only after instance is setup.   If you would like to separate bizx and learning admins, I would suggest taking Admin Overseer type or follow RBP role configuration to authorize normal admin users. Best regards, Fannie Guan

entry.title: ‌🇩🇪‌ Webinar: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance B2B e-Invoicing in Germany
entry.content:
Electronic invoicing requirements in Germany are evolving rapidly. Since January 2025, companies must be able to receive EN 16931-compliant e-invoices. From January 2027, organisations with annual turnover above €800,000 must issue structured e-invoices for domestic B2B transactions, with all companies included from January 2028.

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance supports these requirements, helping organisations manage compliant e-invoicing processes within their SAP landscape.

In this webinar, SAP localization experts will provide:

  • An overview of regulatory requirements and timelines
  • Key capabilities of SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
  • Insights into inbound and outbound e-invoicing processes
  • A live system demonstration

Speakers:
Chi Wan Yuen, Product Manager SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, SAP SE
Sar'a Güzelkaya, Localization Product Manager Germany, SAP SE

This session will be help in German.

Join to stay informed and prepare for upcoming compliance obligations! REGISTER NOW

Curious about how SAP handles SAP Document Reporting Compliance in Germany? Join the Germany - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Work Zone group and hear directly from the experts.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Post Asset With TT 150 with Document Type A...
entry.content:
Hi Mallikarjuna,

Please check the below points in Z02/Z03:

  1. Transaction Type 150 is normally used for asset acquisition/transfer scenarios and must be allowed for the asset class/asset status. SAP confirms transaction types control how FI-AA business transactions are processed. (SAP Help Portal)

  2. Check whether the asset is complete, capitalized, and open for posting in AS03/AS02.

  3. Compare config between working client and Z02/Z03:

    • AO73 / OAYZ – depreciation area and asset class settings

    • AO90 – account determination

    • OBA7 – document type AB settings

    • OB52 – posting period open for asset/G/L

    • OAYR / company code asset settings

    • Asset class allowed transaction type group

  4. If the error is AA834 / “You cannot use this transaction type to post to this asset”, then the likely issue is that transaction type group for TT 150 is not permitted for the asset/asset class. SAP KBA preview also points to allowing the transaction type group for the asset class. (SAP Support Portal)

  5. Also verify whether this is an affiliated company/vendor trading partner scenario. In such cases, SAP Community references using TT 145 or 150 depending on the setup. (SAP Community)

Ask functional/FI-AA team to compare Z02/Z03 with a working client and transport the missing FI-AA configuration. 

 

Regards , 

Abhishek

entry.title: Re: ABAP development under the Clean Core paradigm: From BAPIs to BO and CDS
entry.content:
Excellent breakdown of how ABAP development is evolving under the clean core paradigm. Your explanation of the transition from tightly coupled BAPI-based patterns to BO-based APIs, CDS models, and RAP-aligned extensibility reflects what we’re seeing across large S/4HANA programs. The focus on API stability, semantic data modeling, and upgrade-safe extension points is critical — especially as customers modernize custom code and reduce modification debt.

It would be great to see deeper dives into topics like:

  • mapping legacy BAPI logic into behavior definitions

  • handling complex validations and determinations in RAP

  • strategies for refactoring enhancement-heavy modules into clean core-compatible services

  • governance models for managing custom APIs alongside SAP standard ones

Overall, a very timely and practical contribution for teams navigating the clean core journey.

entry.title: Re: Cannot delete fields from Z* incompletion procedure (...
entry.content:
For user fix, try this:

Go to CBC → Define Incompleteness Procedures.
Do not delete directly from Z2 first.
Check where Z2 is assigned:
Sales document type
Item category
Schedule line/category if applicable
If Z2 is copied from standard and fields are protected/greyed out, create a new custom incompletion procedure, for example ZZ2, instead of modifying Z2.
Copy only required fields into ZZ2.
Assign ZZ2 to the relevant item category / sales document process.
Remove assignment of old Z2 from that item/category.
Save and transport.
Retest by creating a new sales order.

entry.title: User Consumption Report for Particular Time Period
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to get a report of user login consumption for a particular time period.

Example: In a month, how many hours the user has logged in. 

How to get this report?

 

Thanks. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: User Consumption Report for Particular Time Period
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

 

The information of how many hours the users have logged in the system is not available. The system doesn't track or calculate cumulative login hours per user.

Because it is a cloud environment, sessions frequently time out, run in background threads, or stay active via single sign-on (SSO) tokens, meaning duration metrics aren't a reliable or standard KPI recorded in the backend.

There is a pre-delivered standard CDS view that captures Identity and Access Management (IAM) logon metadata.  It doesn't calculate how many hours the users logged in, but if you want a report showing who has logged in, when they last logged in, or active login instances within a period, you can use the CDS View I_IAMBusinessUserLogonDetails.

You can create a Custom CDS View using this CDS as data source. To create a Custom CDS View check documentation SAP Help Portal | Creating Custom CDS Views. 

For more information about Identity and Access Management, check KBA 3212871 - How to get "Identity and Access Management" information - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

 

Best regards,

SAP Support.

entry.title: /users/current/ being appended to URLs after Composable Storefront and SAP Commerce Cloud upgrade
entry.content:
Hi Team, we have upgraded our Composable Storefront and SAP Commerce Cloud to newer versions:

  • Composable Storefront: 2211.37.0 > 221121.7

  • SAP Commerce Cloud: 2211.43 > 2211-jdk21.7

But there is an issue observed change in the OOTB cms APIs - /cms/pages and /cms/components.

BEFORE upgrade:

  • /occ/v2/campbellsretailersspa/cms/pages?PERSON_NAME=en&curr=AUD 

AFTER upgrade:

  • /occ/v2/campbellsretailersspa/users/current/cms/pages?PERSON_NAME=en&curr=AU

As a result, the APIs have started failing. So, the question is whether this additional /users/current/ part can be stripped from the API requests, or is there any other fix available in this regard?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: /users/current/ being appended to URLs after Composab...
entry.content:
Hey there!

This is a technical change introduced in the Composable Storefront version 221121.7 for the OccCmsComponentAdapter and OccCmsPageAdapter. We removed all logic related to the USER_CMS_ENDPOINTS constant and removed usage of FeatureConfigService.

For the OccCmsComponentAdapter, the adapter now always uses the userIdService to determine the current user, and builds the component request URL accordingly.

For the OccCmsPageAdapter, similar to the component adapter, it now always fetches the user ID to build CMS page request URLs.

Now all CMS component and page requests now consistently include the user context in the endpoint URL. The endpoint format is now standardized: /users/{userId}/cms/components and /users/{userId}/cms/pages is always used instead of the legacy /cms/components and /cms/pages format.

The conditional branching based on USER_CMS_ENDPOINTS constant has been eliminated, simplifying the code path. Along with ensuring personalized CMS content is consistently served, based on the authenticated or anonymous user context.

Kind Regards,
Wesley
SAP Support

entry.title: Alteração de codigo de imposto em lançamento advindo do modulo de venda futura
entry.content:
Boa tarde!!

A empresa que trabalha emite nota fiscal de venda futura e posteriormente envia nota de remessa para entrega do produto. 

Atualmente não consigo emitir uma nota de remessa vinculada a uma nota de faturamento simples ( módulo: vedas-> Venda futura) porque no momento de " copiar para - entrega" o sistema não me permite alterar o código de imposto. A nota de remessa deve fazer a contabilização descrita abaixo, pois a nota de simples fatura somente contábil Cliente (Ativo) contra a conta de simples faturamento (Passivo) e está correta devido ao formato de nossa operação. Minha pergunta é: Qual o parâmetro devo fazer para que no momento de copiar uma nota de faturamento simples (módulo: vedas-> Venda futura) o SAP B1 permita a alteração do código de imposto?

Dificuldades no processo:

1. No procedimento de copiar a nota de simples faturamento para entrega, o sistema não deixa alterar o codigo de imposto: Como liberamos o sistema para conseguirmos alterar o codigo de imposto na tela de entrega?

2.  A contabilização na Tela de entrega deve ser a seguinte: 

D - Venda para Entrega Futura (Passivo Circulante) 

C - Receita Bruta de Vendas (Resultado) 

C - Pis a recolher Passivo (Passivo) 

D – Cofins a recolher do passivo (Passivo) 

D - (-) Pis a recolher Passivo (Resultado)

C – (-) Cofins a recolher do passivo (Resultado) 

D - Custo das Mercadorias Vendidas - CMV (Resultado)

C - Estoque de Mercadorias (Ativo Circulante) 

Desde já agradeço. 

Atenciosamente

entry.title: Re: Como integrar um serviço BTP Cloud Foundry a um ambiente S4HANA On-premise
entry.content:
@FabioPagoti boa, gostei do seu comentário.

Sim, o contexto principal desse post seria uma aplicação CAP usando o Node com approuter, e o frontend Fiori (openui5) consumindo fontes Odata, ou seja, na prática vira um serviço que faz o roteamento e também serve arquivos estáticos.

Mas de fato esse cenário também pode ser feito de outras maneiras, como desacoplar o frontend usando o html5 repository, e também pode ser feito o deploy através do MTA.

O foco principal do post era descrever, de forma um pouco mais prática, a arquitetura da integração entre esses 3 pontos: Cloud Foundry, Cloud Connector e S4Hana, por isso não foquei tanto na parte do Node e nem do frontend.

Nas minhas pesquisas, tanto em fóruns quando usando IA, a informação sempre aparece muito fragmentada, o que dificulta o entendimento do cenário de ponta a ponta, daí surgiu a ideia desse blog post.

entry.title: Hands-on Practice for Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA Asset Management
entry.content:
I encountered an issue practicing with Organizational Levels. I need to display a specific Business Partner. I have a set of instructions which I followed:

a) In Fiori Launchpad navigate to Organization >Maintain Business Partner .

b) Select by: Find = Business Partner, BY = Search Term, Search Term 2 = S43*.

c) In the resulting list double-click on your user S43000-##.

d) Check the business partner assigned to your user.

e) Navigate to the Identification tab and show assignment of the personnel number (f.ex. 878 for user S43000-01).

 

Issues Encountered

After following step b), the results did not populate all users with S43000. It started from S43000-31. I need the user S43000-30. 

 

Work around

I had to search for S43000-30 to load this particular user in the search table.

Question

Why am I not able to find the whole users with the wildcard S43*?

entry.title: Re: Display item of content page on master page in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
This has been nicely described. But that indicates we have to modify the Master Template and add peace of script to it.

That will be good when the Master Template is dedicated to the form.

But when the Master Template is used by several Content forms, in fragmented format, that can be an issue to have script in it.

We should push the value from the content form to the master form. That way, regardless of the content form, the value can provide from another input field.

To perform that, we should remove the code from the Master Form and add it to the content area at the "FORM:READY" event of the field that contain the value.

Prerequisite:

1. A Hidden subform, in the content form, contain the field and value to transfer to the Master form

2. Make sure the field contains the value to pass

Form.frmHiddenGlobalFields.InvoiceNumber::ready:layout

xfa.resolveNode("#pageSet[0].mst2.SubsequentPage.frmHeader.FormTitle") = $

 

Regards

entry.title: SAP Developer News, May 21st, 2026
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

2026 Developer Insights Survey Results

  • Blog Post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/the-2026-developer-insights-survey-the-report/ba-p/14391535

UX Introducing the new SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library

  • Library Site: https://fal.cloud.sap/
  • Blog Post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/introducing-the-new-sap-fiori-apps-reference-library/ba-p/14346676

Live Report from Sapphire, Spain

UX Innovation Day in Silicon Valley – June 11th

  • Event Site: hhttps://events.sap.com/us-ux-innovation-day-siliconvalley-2026/en_us/home.html

DYK 17 – Read Only Preview Feature in ABAP Cleaner

  • ABAP Cleaner Project: https://github.com/SAP/abap-cleaner

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 2026 Developer Insights Survey Results

0:54 Introducing the New SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library

2:04 Live Report from Sapphire, Spain

4:25 UX Innovation Day in Silicon valley on June 11th

5:23 DKY #17 Read Only Preview feature in ABAP Cleaner

Transcription

00;00;00;04 - 00;00;06;11
Intro
This is the SAP Developer News for May the 21st, 2026.

00;00;06;14 - 00;00;09;26
Mamikee Kanneh
results of the 2026 SAP Developer Insight Survey are

00;00;09;26 - 00;00;31;10
Mamikee Kanneh
now live, and there are some really interesting trends in this year's report. We're seeing continued growth in AI adoption with developers not only using AI tools in production, but also using them to learn and explore new concepts. We also saw a continued rise in solution and application architects across the community, showing how developer roles are evolving beyond just coding.

00;00;31;15 - 00;00;54;06
Mamikee Kanneh
Now for learning enablement. The report highlights how developers are learning today and the resources they rely on the most. The report also looks at trends across cloud development, developer tooling, languages, frameworks, and the broader SAP developer ecosystem. Now, if you're interested in the results and won't explore the findings in more detail, check out the full report in the link below.

00;00;54;08 - 00;01;23;04
Thomas Jung
In this developer news segment, we want to tell you about a new tool that SAP has recently made available. The Fiori Apps Reference Library. Now this is actually something that from the name you might not know what all it does. It actually breaks down all the different SAP provided pieces of functionality, business functionality like different transaction codes or different applications, and tells you what UI technology that they currently use.

00;01;23;12 - 00;01;51;12
Thomas Jung
This is a question that I've gotten a lot over the years from people wanting to know, well, how can I see what all Fiori UIs are available? But this also shows you the older technologies as well. It lists SAPGUI applications, Web Dynpro, other types. So it can really help you in your process as you're trying to catalog or move to move to the cloud or modernize, particularly your S/4 environment.

00;01;51;12 - 00;02;06;19
Thomas Jung
So this is a great new tool for developers and business experts alike. Be sure to check it out. The link to the tool will be in the show notes as always.

00;02;06;22 - 00;02;20;07
Antonio Maradiaga
We are at the show floor here in Sapphire Madrid. As you can see in the back. We are at the agent lab where it's been quite busy this past couple of days. Lots of interest around Joule Studio. What can you tell us about it? Kevin?

00;02;20;08 - 00;02;40;14
Kevin Riedelsheimer
Antonio, I got to say, everybody's really excited to get their hands on the fresh and brand new Joule Studio. They're very excited about the capabilities of SAP, providing 50 years of business acumen right there in Joule Studio. So they have everything at their fingertips, not only AI assisted coding, but also all of SAP's knowledge right there in the tool.

00;02;40;14 - 00;02;42;02
Kevin Riedelsheimer
So they love that.

00;02;42;04 - 00;02;44;17
Antonio Maradiaga
What about you, Witalij? What have you seen?

00;02;44;19 - 00;03;04;18
Witalij Rudnicki
I don't think that three days here at Sapphire turned me into a marketing person, just like some other colleagues of mine became. I still see this as a technology. It's exciting technology. It's really interesting to learn. There are lots of questions, not only technical questions, but, well, philosophical questions about the future of work, about the future of development.

00;03;04;18 - 00;03;13;23
Witalij Rudnicki
And I must say that during these three days, I had more philosophical discussions than during my previous 25 years in SAP ecosystem.

00;03;13;25 - 00;03;15;27
Antonio Maradiaga
Excellent and Hola.

00;03;16;00 - 00;03;19;13
Josh (Bluebeard) Bentley
SAP community.

00;03;19;16 - 00;03;41;05
Josh (Bluebeard) Bentley
I'm so happy to be back with the developer advocates. We've had an amazing few days here in Madrid. We were at a Stomtisch meetup last night. All of us got there, enjoyed conversation with David and the local team as well as others who came up from Barcelona. Here it has been full the entire show. People coding with Joule Studio using this technology in ways that they've been learning about in the ecosystem, and now seeing it in action in a preview model.

00;03;41;05 - 00;03;58;10
Josh (Bluebeard) Bentley
This is a staging system. It's not fully productive yet. We're hoping to have a sign up for a playground system so people can actually use this in the community as soon as possible. August timeframe right before Q3 when it goes GA. So Joule Studio super popular, a complete reinvent of how SAP is doing the modeling and the building of agents.

00;03;58;10 - 00;04;10;16
Josh (Bluebeard) Bentley
And it's just a great time to be talking about all the stuff happening in this world that we work with. So much cool technology, and I think a great time to talk about even more will be at TechEd. So I'll see you at TechEd in late October in Berlin.

00;04;10;22 - 00;04;26;10
Witalij Rudnicki
And next time you are in Madrid and you are attending Amitabh here. Remember, there are more than one tap rooms here, so check, double check what address you need to be going to say.

00;04;26;12 - 00;04;54;23
Thomas Jung
A few weeks ago, we told you about a upcoming UX Innovation Day in the Silicon Valley area that will be taking place on June 11th, and we thought this would be a good opportunity to remind you of that, because there's still time to register and plan to attend. And coming out of Sapphire last week, it's pretty obvious to everybody that AI is going to have a huge impact on, well, everything in our ecosystem.

00;04;54;23 - 00;05;26;19
Thomas Jung
But the user experience is a big part of that impact. So this session that's being planned on UX innovation will focus on the impact of AI. So if you've got lots of questions or ideas coming out of all the analysis from Sapphire, be sure to attend this session in Silicon Valley. The link to register or find out more details is in the show notes as always.

00;05;26;22 - 00;05;54;06
Sheena M K
Did you know that you can preview your cleaned code using ABAP Cleaner without locking or changing the code? Let me show you how in seconds. First, select any amount of ABAP code in the current code editor in ADT. Next press Ctrl+Shift+five or select Menu ->Source Code->Show->Read Only Preview with ABAP Cleaner. This opens the interactive ABAP Cleaner UI, but you can notice that the apply button is not available.

00;05;54;13 - 00;06;03;15
Sheena M K
Here you can not only get a preview of your code with the suggestions from ABAP Cleaner, but also copy the code from the clipboard or file without making any

entry.title: Is SAP Still a Software Company? Reflections from Sapphire 2026
entry.content:
Like many of you, we attended SAP Sapphire Orlando 2026 last week. Before it, our minds were already set on the most likely topics the conversations would revolve around. We thought about the usual when it comes to enterprise AI themes: copilots, new generative capabilities, maybe the launching of something unexpected. Of course, those topics were covered, and we were right to assume that they would be the center of the event. However, the keynote didn’t waste any time and soon revealed that we were there for something much bigger; we were there to witness the first steps towards a bigger strategic shift that is taking place inside SAP. The discussion was no longer just about adding AI features into enterprise software, but about redefining the role ERP systems could play in the next generation of enterprise AI execution.

Over the past two years, most organizations have been implementing copilots that help employees be more productive. For instance, employees can send emails faster, write meeting minutes faster, and look for information easier. However, once enterprises attempt to implement AI on a larger scale, it becomes clear how difficult it is for businesses to actually make things work. Recommendations can be made but putting them into practice in an environment full of disparate systems, workflows, approvals, and data sources can be extremely hard.

This is precisely why SAP's approach to AI seems so unique. Instead of presenting AI as another layer separate from the core of enterprise operations, SAP is attempting to integrate AI into the very systems where business processes take place. As one of the speakers emphasized during their keynote presentation, ERP is "the brain of every company." This idea formed the basis for all the presentations at Sapphire. SAP's view on enterprise AI is that it becomes much more valuable when it operates with process awareness, governance, semantic business context, and operational data.

One of the key announcements made at Sapphire is SAP Business AI Platform. Surprisingly, SAP did not introduce it as "another AI offering". This product is positioned as an architectural layer connecting enterprise AI to operational systems and business processes. The purpose here is to merge ERP process knowledge, Business Data Cloud, AI orchestration, governance frameworks, and semantic business context into a single enterprise AI layer for executing actions, not just giving suggestions.

Another dominant topic at the Sapphire event was moving from AI assistants to operational AI agents. SAP introduced hundreds of AI agents and assistants for Finance, Procurement, Supply Chain, HCM, and Customer Experience processes. However, the number of them was not really the point of their announcement. Much greater attention was paid to orchestration. SAP clearly communicated that enterprise AI will not succeed with isolated copilots, but rather with integrated AI systems that can interact in operational processes. In other words, the real challenge today is not just implementing AI solutions. The goal is to achieve operationalizing them within the enterprise.

Especially fascinating about this approach is its implications for enterprise architecture going forward. Traditionally, enterprises have always treated enterprise software as an assembly of individual platforms that can be integrated. But now, AI is starting to reveal the limitations of such an approach. The more fragmented the operational environment becomes, the less effective it is for AI algorithms to work. This is likely the reason behind SAP’s long focus on topics such as Business Data Cloud, semantic layer, governance models, and process orchestration. It looks like SAP believes that the future success of enterprise AI lies not in how well the individual models perform but rather in the level of their connectivity and operational awareness.

This also signals an important shift for SAP partners and customers. Until recently, discussions around digital transformation revolved around implementation, migration, and modernization. But as enterprise AI becomes more prominent, the focus is shifting to operational readiness. Clean data structures, process standardization, governance models, and ERP systems become prerequisites for successful AI adoption rather than just IT requirements. In a way, Sapphire 2026 suggests that the most capable adopters of AI are not necessarily those experimenting with the greatest number of copilots, but those with the most robust operational foundations to support them.

Following Sapphire 2026, it becomes increasingly clear that SAP tries to evolve beyond being defined as a software company. It seeks to position itself as the operational layer for AI, merging ERP logic, enterprise data, governance, and AI orchestration into a single architecture. If this vision is going to materialize is yet to be seen, but the direction is hard to deny. SAP believes that the next wave of competitive advantages in enterprise AI does not lie in the strength of the AI models but in the ownership of the operational systems that execute AI-driven business decisions.

entry.title: SAP Commerce Cloud SSR - Intermittent HttpErrorResponse status 0 / UND_ERR_SOCKET during OCC calls
entry.content:
SAP Commerce Cloud SSR - Intermittent HttpErrorResponse status 0 / UND_ERR_SOCKET during OCC calls

Hi everyone,

we are experiencing intermittent issues in an SAP Commerce Cloud SSR (Composable Storefront SSR / Angular Universal) environment.

During SSR rendering, some OCC requests randomly fail with:

  • HttpErrorResponse

  • status: 0

  • statusText: 'Unknown Error'

  • TypeError: fetch failed

  • UND_ERR_SOCKET

  • SocketError: other side closed

The failing request is a standard OCC CMS components call (also on other standard calls such as /languages).

Relevant log excerpt:

[cause]: Le {
headers: t { headers: Map(0) {}, normalizedNames: Map(0) {}, lazyInit: undefined, lazyUpdate: null },
status: 0,
statusText: 'Unknown Error',
url: 'https://api.store.ro/occ/v2/store/users/anonymous/cms/components?fields=DEFAULT&currentPage=0&pageSize=3&componentIds=BannerC&lang=en&curr=EUR',

error: TypeError: fetch failed
at node:internal/deps/undici/undici:14976:13

at /opt/dynatrace/oneagent-paas/agent/bin/1.335.58.20260423-145051/any/nodejs/nodejsagent.js:1:427519
at AsyncLocalStorage.run (node:internal/async_local_storage/async_hooks:80:14) {
[cause]: SocketError: other side closed
at TLSSocket.<anonymous> (node:internal/deps/undici/undici:6436:28)

code: 'UND_ERR_SOCKET',
socket: { localAddress: 'xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx', localPort: 52356, remoteAddress: 'xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx', remotePort: 443, remoteFamily: 'IPv4', timeout: undefined, bytesWritten: 483, bytesRead: 0 },
[Symbol(undici.error.UND_ERR)]: true,
[Symbol(undici.error.UND_ERR_SOCKET)]: true
}
}
}
}

What is interesting is:

  • the remote side closes the TLS socket

  • no HTTP response code is returned

  • bytesRead = 0

  • SSR eventually returns HTTP 500

  • the issue is intermittent (same request sometimes returns 200)

  • WAF is disabled on api endpoint

From our perspective, this does not look like a functional OCC problem or missing CMS data, but more like:

  • firewall/WAF interruption

  • load balancer reset

  • ingress/network policy issue

  • TLS/socket termination upstream

Questions:

  1. Has anyone experienced similar intermittent UND_ERR_SOCKET / status 0 issues in SSR environments?

  2. Are there known SAP Commerce Cloud infrastructure/network limitations or timeout behaviors that can cause this?

  3. Could this be related to CCv2 ingress, WAF, CDN, or internal routing?

  4. Is there any recommended retry strategy or SSR-side mitigation for dropped outbound OCC calls?

Environment:

  • SAP Commerce Cloud CCv2 2211 jdk21.9

  • Composable Storefront SSR

  • Node.js fetch/undici stack

  • Dynatrace enabled

Any insights would be appreciated.

entry.title: Re: How do Customizing Tenant and Development Tenant work...
entry.content:
Hello!

In classic 4-system on-premise landscape, configuration and development are co-located in one Dev system and moved together. In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, they are separated by design (configuration vs. extensibility are governed differently), but arrive both in Test and Production. What you see in CBC as two "deployment targets" are two distinct tenants within the Development tier of the 3-system landscape. The overall landscape is:

a. Development System

a.1.  Customizing Tenant 

a.2. Development Tenant 

b. Test System

c. Production System

They are parallel work streams within the same tier, not separate landscapes and share the same Test and Production system (no independent transport landscapes). There is only one Test system and one Production system in the 3-system landscape. 

Customizing Tenant: Configuration changes are transported using CBC's own transport framework, specifically the Deploy Scope and Organizational Structure activity and subsequent configuration releases. SAP manages the transport infrastructure; you trigger releases through the CBC UI.

Development Tenant:  Custom developments are packaged and released via standard Software Collection / Release Transport mechanisms.

There is no direct transport between the two tenants themselves, are independent work streams that converge only when both reach the Test system. This means integration testing (ensuring config and custom code work together) happens in the Test system, not in Development.

UAT is conducted once, in the Test system (not in either Development tenant). Business users should never be testing in the Development tenants (same principle as in on-premise: you don't do UAT in the Dev system).

The workflow is:

Consultant configures in Customizing Tenant → transports to Test

Developer builds extensions in Development Tenant → transports to Test

Both streams arrive in Test → business users perform UAT once

Sign-off → transport to Production

 

Additional details can be founded here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/aa60b129af7b4ce8ae052618c8315d29.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/e022623ec1fc4d61abb398e411670200.html?locale=en-US

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/transport-management-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-3-system-landscape/ba-p/13625058

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition

entry.title: Re: Bank statement processing - No technical account requ...
entry.content:
Hello!

The technical/interim GL account being posted to during bank statement upload is actually part of the standard SAP bank reconciliation process. The way it's designed to work is:

  • When a payment is made in SAP - the interim/clearing GL is debited
  • When the bank statement is uploaded and matched - hat same interim GL gets cleared

By design, this account should be impacted and should net to zero once reconciliation is complete.

Could you help clarify what you're actually seeing?

  1. Is the interim / technical GL being posted to but NOT getting cleared after the bank statement upload?
  2. Or is it being posted to on transactions where you wouldn't expect it?
  3. Or is the concern something else entirely ?

The Automate button is part of a feature called Advanced Bank Statement Automation, which in SAP is bundled under scope item 4X8. But starting with August 2024 release CE2408 was included in the standard package without any additional activation needed.

  1. If you are on CE2408 or later the button should already be available. If it's still not showing, it's most likely a user authorization issue, meaning your user profile may not have the right roles/permissions assigned to see it.
  2. If you are on a release older than CE2408  you would need scope item 4X8 activated.

Checking scope item 1MV (Cash Application Integration) won't help here as that's a completely different feature unrelated to the Automate button.

entry.title: Re: Automatic payment block required for employee invoice...
entry.content:
Hello!

Primary recommendation is creating grouping key on employee vendor master.

How it works: A Grouping Key assigned to the employee vendor master instructs the Automatic Payment Program to cluster all open items, both invoices and credit notes, under the same grouping bucket before calculating any payment. This ensures the credit note offsets the invoice rather than being evaluated in isolation.

Outcome: The APP will net the invoice and credit note together, paying only the remaining balance and eliminating exception queue entries.

 

Second recommendation will be to align payment terms on credit notes. If credit notes are posted without payment terms or with terms that differ from the corresponding invoices, SAP evaluates them on a different due date and they fall outside the active selection window. This can be enforced through default payment terms configured in the employee vendor master Manage Business Partner.

Outcome: Invoices and credit notes become due at the same time, allowing SAP  to evaluate and net them within the same run.

 

Supplementary can be used payment block as process control. As an additional safeguard, a standard payment block can be applied to expense invoices at the time of posting. The block is only released once the corresponding credit note has been posted and is available in the system.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, standard delivered payment blocks are available and can be applied manually during document entry or via the Change Documents app.

Outcome: Prevents premature payment of invoices and ensures invoices and credit notes are always processed together.

entry.title: One Data Catalog: AI-Powered Metadata and Search for Better Data Governance
entry.content:
Manual metadata management can hold data governance initiatives back. AI-generated tagging, classification, descriptions, documentation, and intelligent search are changing the game. And the SAP Datasphere — a component of SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) — One Data Catalog is leading the way.

The Governance Gap in Your Data Catalog

Data governance is only as strong as the metadata that supports it. Without accurate tags, classifications, descriptions, and documentation, governance policies cannot be applied consistently, sensitive data goes unprotected, and compliance obligations become difficult to demonstrate.

The problem? Maintaining accurate metadata with business context across thousands of data assets has traditionally required enormous manual effort — effort that most organizations simply cannot sustain. And even when metadata exists, finding the right data asset quickly and confidently remains a challenge for business users, analysts, and data stewards alike.

The result is incomplete catalogs, inconsistent classifications, outdated documentation, and governance gaps that create real regulatory and business risk.  This is compounded by a search experience that sends users down dead ends or, worse, toward the wrong data entirely.

AI-generated metadata and intelligent search close that gap. And the SAP Datasphere’s One Data Catalog is built to make it happen automatically and consistently.

A Governance Challenge That Will Sound Familiar

Consider a global pharmaceutical company preparing for a regulatory audit. The auditors want to see exactly what customer and patient data the organization holds, how it is classified, who has access to it, and how it has been used in reporting and analytics.

The data governance team knows the data exists — spread across SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, and several third-party systems — but pulling together a comprehensive, accurate picture is a painful, weeks-long manual exercise. Datasets are inconsistently tagged. Sensitive fields are not always clearly classified. Documentation is incomplete and outdated. And when business analysts need data for compliance reporting, they often end up using unverified spreadsheets because finding the right governed dataset takes too long.

This is not a technology failure. It is a metadata failure — and it is far more common than most organizations care to admit.

With SAP Datasphere’s One Data Catalog, this scenario plays out very differently. Here is how.

Five Ways AI Strengthens Data Governance

1. AI-Generated Tagging — Consistent Governance Across Every Asset

When the pharmaceutical company connects its SAP S/4HANA and third-party systems to SAP BDC, the One Data Catalog can begin to analyze every data asset. Clinical trial datasets are consistently tagged with “Regulated Data”, ‘Clinical”, and “Confidential”. Financial records receive “Finance” and “Sensitive” tags. Customer records are tagged with “Customer Data” and “PII”.

Governance Benefit: Uniform tag coverage means governance policies are applied consistently across the entire data landscape — no gaps, no exceptions. When auditors ask what regulated data the organization holds, the answer is immediate and comprehensive.

2. AI-Generated Classification — Protecting Sensitive Data

The One Data Catalog can auto-generate classification for each field by sensitivity level — Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted — and regulatory category — PII, GDPR-relevant, HIPAA-relevant, financial data.

In our pharmaceutical example, patient identifiers, treatment records, and clinical outcomes are auto-classified as “HIPAA-Relevant” and “Restricted”.  A field that might have sat undetected in a lightly governed dataset is now protected before anyone has had the opportunity to misuse it.

Governance Benefit: Sensitive data is identified, protected, and governed automatically — reducing compliance risk and eliminating the danger of manual misclassification across complex, multi-system data landscapes.

3. AI-Generated Descriptions — Reducing Data Misuse Through Clarity

The pharmaceutical company's data landscape includes dozens of technical SAP table names that mean nothing to compliance analysts or business users. Without clear descriptions, users make dangerous assumptions — using the wrong dataset for regulatory reporting or misinterpreting field values in ways that compromise data integrity.

The One Data Catalog can automatically generate plain-language descriptions for every data asset, table, and field. A technical table containing adverse event reporting data is automatically described as: “This dataset contains records of adverse events reported during clinical trials, including event type, severity, patient demographics, and reporting dates. It is subject to regulatory reporting requirements and restricted to authorized clinical and compliance personnel."

Governance Benefit: Clear, accurate descriptions ensure data is used correctly and in the right context — reducing misuse, improving data literacy, and giving compliance teams confidence that analysts are working with the right data.  And it saves time!

4. AI-Generated Documentation — Audit Readiness Without the Scramble

When the regulatory audit arrives, the pharmaceutical company's governance team does not scramble. The One Data Catalog has been continuously assembling and maintaining comprehensive documentation for every data asset — combining AI-generated descriptions with lineage information, quality metrics, access logs, and governance metadata.

Auditors receive complete data dictionaries, lineage summaries showing exactly where data originates and how it flows, quality reports demonstrating data reliability, and access records showing who has interacted with sensitive datasets. What previously took weeks to compile manually is now available on demand — current, complete, and credible.

Governance Benefit: Continuous, automated documentation means organizations are always audit-ready — demonstrating compliance with confidence rather than scrambling to compile records after the fact. And again, the manual effort in creating documentation is all but eliminated.

5. AI-Assisted Search — Guiding Users to Trusted, Governed Data

Even the most well-governed catalog delivers limited value if users cannot find what they need quickly and confidently. In our pharmaceutical example, compliance analysts previously resorted to unverified spreadsheets simply because finding the right governed dataset took too long. Every time that happened, governance was bypassed at the most critical point — consumption.

SAP Datasphere’s AI-assisted search transforms this experience entirely. A compliance analyst preparing a regulatory submission types "patient adverse event data last 24 months" into the search bar. Within seconds, they receive a curated list of relevant, governed datasets — enriched with sensitivity classifications and usage guidelines.

Critically, search results respect the analyst's access permissions. They see only what they are authorized to use and every result they see has been tagged, classified, and documented by AI, giving them confidence that the data is appropriate for their compliance use case.

The days of bypassing governed data because it was too hard to find are over.

Governance Benefit: AI-assisted search steers users toward trusted, governed data assets at the point of discovery.

AI and Human Governance Working Together

It is important to emphasize that AI-generated metadata and search capabilities do not replace human governance judgment, they empower it.

In our pharmaceutical example, data stewards review and approve AI-generated classifications for the most sensitive clinical datasets, adding nuanced context that only human experts can provide. User feedback continuously refines AI accuracy, so the catalog becomes smarter and more reliable over time.

The result is a governance model where AI handles scale, consistency, and discoverability while humans provide oversight, expertise, and institutional knowledge — a partnership that delivers governance outcomes neither could achieve alone.

The Governance Impact – Capabilities and Governance Outcome

  • AI Tagging - Consistent policy application across all assets
  • AI Classification - Sensitive data protection and compliance
  • AI Descriptions - Reduced data misuse through improved understanding
  • AI Documentation - Continuous audit readiness, knowledge preservation, and reduction time, errors, and incompleteness in manual data entry
  • AI-Assisted Search - Governed data consumption at the point of discovery

Conclusion

The pharmaceutical company in our scenario is not unique. Organizations across every industry face the same fundamental challenge: data governance requires metadata that is complete, consistent, accurate, and current — and a search experience that actively guides users toward trusted, governed data — at a scale that manual processes simply cannot achieve.

 Watch a sales oriented example in action

SAP Datasphere’s One Data Catalog meets that challenge head-on. By combining AI-generated tagging, classification, descriptions, documentation, and intelligent search into a single, integrated platform, it delivers governance that is proactive, scalable, and continuous — rather than reactive, incomplete, and perpetually behind.

When governance is powered by intelligent, automated metadata and search, sensitive data is protected, compliance is demonstrable, data is used correctly, and users are naturally guided toward trusted sources. That is not just better metadata management or a better search experience — that is better governance, end to end.

Additional Resources

Interested in learning more as to how SAP Datasphere as a part of SAP Business Data Cloud can strengthen your organization's data governance program?

  • Watch the demo:  Improve data discovery using AI in SAP Datasphere
  • Get the free trial: SAP Datasphere
  • Get started with the SAP Datasphere tutorial: Get Ready for Your SAP Datasphere Trial Mission

The data will finally be delivered to the SFTP server and synchronized back to the HANA system.(Ariba->MDI->Cloud Integration)
Is this method achievable?

entry.title: Re: Planned Replenishment to fill in terms of pallet. not...
entry.content:
Good day @TilakJoshyChenna 

Your question wasn't specific but below you will find some details.

You should maintain th replenishment master data in pallet multiples. Planned replenishment as you may know, calculates the replenishment quantity from the minimum and maximum quantities you maintained, and where a minimum replenishment quantity exists, the system rounds the replenishment quantity to a multiple of that value.

The practical setup is for you to maintain the product’s pallet alternative unit of measure correctly, for example 1 PAL = 48PC, and then maintain the Minimum Quantity, Maximum Quantity, and Minimum Replenishment Quantity in pallet UoM or in quantities equal to full pallet multiples. See KBA 3619401 for more insights.

If your replenishment task is taking stock from the wrong source storage type, check replenishment source determination. KBA 3075567 provides good explanation on how to manage that situation.

Here is what I suggest as a next step:

  • Check the product UoM in Manage Product Master Data.                                
  • Maintain the storage type replenishment quantities in pallet multiples
  • Verify the fixed bin setup in Assign Fixed Storage Bins                                                                                                    
  • Run the replenishment through Schedule Replenishment                                                                                            
  • Confirm the generated warehouse task in Warehouse Monitor or Confirm Warehouse Tasks.                                    

Also, not that you should not treat the maximum quantity in Assing Fixed Storage Bins should as a putaway capacity control. KBA 3671670 explains why.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Warehouse and Transportation Synchronization in SAP S/4HANA Using SAP EWM and TM
entry.content:
Hi, @Johnny_Li  Thanks for reading and sharing your valuable update. Just to update, in Standard SAP TM, freight order (FO) status is updated after vehicle departure/checkpoint departure. This needs either Yard management or without Yard management (with TU departure). 
Moreover, Standard SAP TM (freight order) status can also be updated at “Finish Loading” using "With early update functionality". 
In this document, I did not use Yard management or TU departure, however I have used "Finish loading", so using early update's functionality, Freight order status was updated successfully.
Thanks again for your comments!

entry.title: Re: How to disable Manager and Self Assignment of items w...

entry.title: Beyond UAT: Why System Integration Test is the True Backbone of SAP S/4HANA Success
entry.content:
Introduction:

The most famous testing level applied in all SAP testing cycles is the User Acceptance Test (UAT). However, there are other critical testing levels that discover a multitude of issues before reaching the UAT phase—most notably, the System Integration Test (SIT). SIT plays a vital role in securing data governance and ensuring end-to-end data integrity. In this article, we will dive deep into this testing level, understanding its core benefits, and exploring how it works.

Defining SIT: An ISTQB Perspective in the SAP Landscape

According to the ISTQB (International Software Testing Qualifications Board) syllabus, System Integration Testing (SIT) is designed to test the interactions between systems or components. This means focusing directly on the interfaces and data flows where the most critical business risks typically reside.

In an SAP context, SIT is the phase where testers transition from evaluating isolated components to validating the technical integration between various modules, or between SAP and external third-party systems. This ensures that data integrity and functional logic remain intact as transactions travel through the entire enterprise ecosystem.

Importance of SIT in SAP Transformations

During complex migration phases—such as moving to SAP S/4HANA—one of the most common issues testers encounter involves data mismatches between modules. For instance, consider a scenario where data flows from a legacy third-party procurement system into the SAP Finance (FI) module, leading to rounding differences or tax calculation mismatches.

The Technical Challenge: During SIT, testers might notice a critical disconnect. While the Goods Receipt (GR) within Materials Management (MM) processes successfully, the subsequent Accounting Document (FI) fails to post. The root cause is often a minor currency conversion error at the API or middleware layer, which triggers a balance mismatch in the clearing account.

The Solution & Value: Instead of simply patching the data to pass the immediate test case, a strategic SIT approach involves performing a deep trace. Testers can work with developers to refine the error-handling framework and implement robust validation checks at the middleware level. This level of thorough investigation not only resolves the immediate bug but prevents hundreds of potential operational failures in the production environment.

The figure below shows a comparative view of how SIT identifies integration failures (like currency mismatches) at the technical layer, ensuring a smooth, defect-free experience for Business Users during subsequent User Acceptance Testing (UAT).

SIT is successful only when it proves that:

  • Data Integrity: Information remains consistent as it travels from one module to another.
  • Interface Reliability: All RICEFW objects and standard APIs perform under end-to-end scenarios.

 

System Integration Test vs. User Acceptance Test: Understanding the Boundary

One of the most common reasons for project delays is the "blurring" of lines between System Integration Test and User Acceptance Test. If we don’t define where one ends and the other begins, the project risks falling into a cycle of re-testing.

While they may seem similar, the objectives are fundamentally different:

  • The Technical vs. The Functional: SIT is performed by functional consultants and technical testers to ensure the system is technically sound. UAT, on the other hand, is performed by Business Key Users to ensure the system meets their daily operational needs.
  • The Environment: SIT usually happens in a controlled QA environment where we can simulate various "negative" scenarios. UAT should happen in a environment that mirrors production, focusing on "happy path" and real-life business scenarios.
  • The Goal: The goal of SIT is to find bugs in the integration logic. The goal of UAT is to gain formal acceptance that the system is ready for the business to us.

The Real Cost of  Fast-tracking  SIT

  • It is a common scenario in SAP projects: the Go-Live date is approaching, timelines are tight, and SIT is the first phase to be squeezed. On paper, it looks like a shortcut to save time. In reality, it’s a high-interest technical debt.
  • This isn't just an opinion; it's a proven principle in software economics known as the "Cost of Change" curve. According to industry standards (like those established by Barry Boehm), the cost of fixing a defect increases significantly as it moves through the lifecycle. Shifting integration issues into UAT doesn't just triple the technical effort—it creates a "Trust Gap."
  • When a Key User—who is already juggling their daily job with project tasks—encounters a system crash or a broken document flow, their confidence in S/4HANA is shaken. It’s important to remember that while we can recover technical time, recovering the trust of the Business Users is far more difficult. A robust SIT isn't a delay; it’s an investment in project credibility.

Conclusion

  • System Integration Test is not an optional phase to be compressed when time is tight. It is the project's reality check. By ensuring that the end-to-end business logic is sound, we aren't just finding bugs; we are protecting the project’s reputation and the users' trust.

In my upcoming articles, let's go deeper in Testing Cycle to see how other levels of tests impact SAP Quality .

for more information you can check:

  • ISTQB Certified Tester Foundation Level (CTFL) Syllabus
  • SAP Activate Methodology - Realize Phase (Integration Testing Workstream).

entry.title: Re: How to reduce/adjust the excess depreciation posted i...
entry.content:
Good day @SANDEEPGHL 

Thank you for your question.

For you to reduce already posted excess depreciation without reversing the original depreciation document, you can use Post Write-Up, not Post Depreciation Manually.  A write-up increases the book of the asset and is the relevant correction when you have accumulated too high depreciation.

You do not need to create a new depreciation key for this correction. A depreciation kex controls future planned depreciation calculation, while you can handle the correction of already posted excess depreciation through an asset transaction posting. See KBA 3640692 for more insights on asset transactions.

As a next step, I suggest you check the asset in Asset Balances with key figure group ABS_POSTED, calculate the excess depreciation amount,                                                                                         

post the same amount using Post Write-Up 

and then validate the corrected posted values again in Asset Balances. KBA 3683613 will support you here.

If the write-up cannot be posted and the system issues AA402, check whether the transaction type is appropriate for the affected depreciation area. That error is explained in KBA 3666481 and it points you to various points you should check.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris
